purpose: Curated procedural memory for Aura changelog media inference, seeding, capture framing, and quality review.
curationPolicy: Store reusable lessons only; do not store screenshots, traces, secrets, or one-off feature hacks.
Promote a lesson only after it improves inference, seeding, capture, or quality review across at least one real or replayed changelog media run.
Keep lessons surface-oriented and capability-oriented instead of selector-oriented.
Do not use lessons to bypass quality gates; failed media must still be omitted from the public changelog.
Prefer deleting stale lessons over accumulating low-confidence memory.

mediaSignals: A changelog entry says a model is available, selectable, added to the picker, or visible in the chat composer.
The changed files touch the model catalog and chat input/model picker UI.
skipSignals: Provider pricing, routing, benchmark, or model catalog plumbing with no explicit user-visible picker/menu claim.

requiredState: A seeded agent chat session is selected.
The chat composer model picker is open.
The requested model label is visible in the picker and selected control.
proofBoundary: The new model label is visible as a selectable option.
The nearby selected model control confirms this is the chat composer picker.
contextBoundary: Keep enough local chat composer context to show the picker belongs to Aura Agents.
Global app header is helpful but not required for compact control proof.

captureGuidance: For compact pickers or dropdowns, use a focused frame where the label and selected control are crisp.
If the compact picker offers Show all or More, expand it when the current teaser does not provide enough context.
Do not reject solely because a compact picker does not fill most of the branded canvas.
qualityGuidance: Pass compact control media when the changed label/control is crisp and the local product context makes the surface understandable.
Reject if the new model label is clipped, unreadable, absent, or shown outside desktop Aura UI.

examples: GPT-5.5 available in the chat model picker
DeepSeek V4 Pro available in the chat model picker

mediaSignals: A changelog entry describes image-to-3D generation, persisted 3D projects, generated models, or a WebGL/model viewer.
The changed files touch AURA 3D project, image, asset gallery, or model preview surfaces.
skipSignals: 3D backend, dependency, or storage plumbing with no visible project, source image, generated model, or viewer claim.

requiredState: A seeded AURA 3D project is selected.
A source image or generated model preview is visible.
The viewer/model surface is open when the changelog mentions 3D model or WebGL viewer.
proofBoundary: Show the generated 3D/model preview or source-image-to-model flow evidence.
Avoid empty project, pick-a-project, or placeholder gallery states.
contextBoundary: Keep the AURA 3D title, selected project, asset/gallery controls, or viewer panel visible around the proof.

captureGuidance: Seed realistic demo project/image/model state before capture; do not capture empty 3D canvases.
Prefer the generated model/viewer area with nearby AURA 3D context over an isolated thumbnail.
qualityGuidance: Reject empty galleries, placeholder canvases, loading viewers, or isolated thumbnails without product context.
Pass when a generated model/viewer or source image workflow is clearly visible and not clipped.

evidence.source: implemented seeded capture bridge support and prior local media experiments
examples: New AURA 3D app with image to 3D model flow and WebGL viewer
3D projects, images, and models persist across sessions

skipSignals: The only visible proof would be an empty list, placeholder panel, loading UI, login wall, or default state.

requiredState: If the feature needs data to be visible, seed realistic demo data before capture.
proofBoundary: The feature itself must be visible, not merely the screen where it would appear later.
contextBoundary: Keep the smallest recognizable product context around populated proof.

captureGuidance: Do not publish media for empty/default screens even if route navigation succeeds.
If seeding cannot create visible proof, omit media and leave the changelog text-only.
qualityGuidance: Reject login, loading, error, placeholder, empty, or unseeded states.

evidence.source: changelog media quality gate failures and hardening
